privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic dataset containing 25 biology and medicine papers intentionally seeded into a Zotero library. Verified facts: (1) RNA interference was discovered by Fire and Mello, published in Nature in 1998. (2) CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing in human cells was first demonstrated by the Zhang lab in 2013 (published in Science and Nature Protocols). (3) Cerebral organoid technology was developed by Lancaster et al., published in Nature in 2013. (4) Craig L. Peterson is a confirmed researcher on chromatin remodeling and gene regulation. (5) Alan R. Saltiel conducted landmark research on insulin signaling and glucose metabolism, published in Nature in 2001. (6) George A. Calin is a leading researcher on microRNAs in cancer biology. (7) Noboru Mizushima is a leading researcher on autophagy and cellular mechanisms. (8) Extensive peer-reviewed literature exists on all five abstract placeholder topics (telomere dynamics, antibiotic resistance, gut microbiome, immune checkpoint blockade, and protein structure prediction using deep learning). Several claimed paper titles do not match exactly with verified literature sources.
